work_items.summary: Implemented signals_log.jsonl — a JSONL file that appends every scored symbol's 9-dimension breakdown after each scan cycle. Fixes the critical data gap identified in the baseline performance audit: per-dimension IC was uncomputable because dimension scores were never persisted.
work_items.root_cause: signals.py already produced score_breakdown (9 dimensions). bot.py already passed it as signal_scores to execute_buy. But no code ever wrote scored symbols to a persistent log. trades.json only records the aggregate score on traded symbols; it never captured all scanned symbols or their dimension breakdowns.
work_items.resolution: Added SIGNALS_LOG_FILE constant to learning.py. Added log_signal_scan(scored, regime) function that appends one JSONL line per symbol per scan (ts, scan_id, symbol, score, price, regime, vix, score_breakdown, disabled_dims). Added signals_log path to config.py. Called log_signal_scan in bot.py immediately after score_universe(). Added 7 tests in TestSignalScanLogging. Added log_signal_scan stub to test_bot.py learning stub.
work_items.notes: Selection bias: signals_log.jsonl only captures above-threshold symbols (score_universe filters before returning). Below-threshold scores are not logged. Document this in any IC analysis — the observable distribution is right-truncated. To fix this properly, score_universe would need to return all scored symbols, not just those above threshold.
